comment: Default-FAIL verification contract for the dogfood-validation task. DOGFOOD FINDING (see reports/implementation/T-011.md): the medium-tier required-set {lint,typecheck,build,...} is calibrated for compiled application code. This repository is shell + Markdown + JSON with no lint/typecheck/build toolchain, and T-011 ships a JSON spec artifact + gate-run logs. lint/typecheck/build are therefore honestly waived (required:false + waiver_reason) rather than backed by fabricated evidence; check-contract correctly reports them as tier-rule findings. Verification is via the deterministic gates and shell test suites.

checks.waiver_reason: Shell+Markdown+JSON repo: no lint toolchain wired (no eslint/ruff/shellcheck target). DOGFOOD FINDING: medium tier requires lint:required=true; not satisfiable without fabrication on this repo. Verification is via deterministic gates + shell suites.
No typed language in this change (JSON + Markdown). Not applicable.
Gate-suite integration is exercised by eval.tests.sh Scenario 9 (recorded under acceptance-tests).
No build step for a shell/markdown plugins repo. DOGFOOD FINDING: medium tier requires build:required=true; not applicable here.
No runnable app; CLI gates are exercised directly and recorded in T-011.self-gates.log.
No behavioral code change; not a refactor task.
